---
title: "federal"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-14"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# ✅ FIX: Load the dataset here so ALL chunks below can see it
# Update this path to match where your .rda file actually lives
load("C:/Users/nomoo/OneDrive/Documents/ICPSR_39270-V2/ICPSR_39270/DS0003/39270-0003-Data.rda")
library(tidyr)
```



```{r}
library(dplyr)

# ==========================================
# 1. Feature Engineering: All-in-One Step
# ==========================================
model_data <- da39270.0003 %>%
  filter(ORI == "WA0130000") %>%
  mutate(
    # THE TARGET: Did an arrest happen?
    is_cleared = as.factor(ifelse(RECSARR > 0, "Cleared", "Not Cleared")),
    
    # OFFENSE COUNT: Single vs. Multiple
    OFFENSE_GROUP = case_when(
      as.numeric(RECSOFS) == 1 ~ "Single Offense",
      as.numeric(RECSOFS) > 1  ~ "Multiple Offenses",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    
    # TIME OF DAY: Grouped into Police Patrol Shifts
    hour_num = as.numeric(V20111),
    SHIFT = case_when(
      hour_num >= 6 & hour_num < 14 ~ "Day Shift (0600-1359)",
      hour_num >= 14 & hour_num < 22 ~ "Swing Shift (1400-2159)",
      hour_num >= 22 | hour_num < 6  ~ "Graveyard Shift (2200-0559)",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown Time"
    ),
    
    # VICTIM AGE: Grouped into life stages
    age_num = as.numeric(V40181),
    AGE_GROUP = case_when(
      age_num < 18 ~ "Juvenile (<18)",
      age_num >= 18 & age_num < 65 ~ "Adult (18-64)",
      age_num >= 65 ~ "Senior (65+)",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown Age"
    ),
    
    # CRIME TYPE: Keeping the full text label!
    CRIME_CATEGORY = as.character(V20061)
  ) %>%
  
  # NOW we drop the raw data, keeping only our clean features
  select(is_cleared, OFFENSE_GROUP, SHIFT, AGE_GROUP, CRIME_CATEGORY)

# ==========================================
# 2. View Clearance Rates by Offense Group
# ==========================================
offense_summary <- model_data %>%
  group_by(OFFENSE_GROUP) %>%
  summarize(
    Total_Incidents = n(),
    Cleared_Count = sum(is_cleared == "Cleared"),
    Clearance_Rate_Pct = round((Cleared_Count / Total_Incidents) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Clearance_Rate_Pct))

print(offense_summary)


library(GGally)

# ==========================================
# Generate the Categorical Pairs Plot
# ==========================================
plot_data_categorical <- model_data %>%
  select(is_cleared, OFFENSE_GROUP, SHIFT, AGE_GROUP) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.factor)) %>%
  drop_na()

ggpairs(
  plot_data_categorical, 
  title = "Grant County Sheriff: Categorical Variables Pairs Plot",
  mapping = aes(color = is_cleared, fill = is_cleared, alpha = 0.6)
)
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Calculate stats by Crime Category
crime_rank <- model_data %>%
  group_by(CRIME_CATEGORY) %>%
  summarize(
    Total = n(),
    Clearance_Rate = sum(is_cleared == "Cleared") / n()
  ) %>%
  filter(Total >= 10) %>%
  arrange(desc(Clearance_Rate))

# Create the Plot
ggplot(crime_rank, aes(x = reorder(CRIME_CATEGORY, Clearance_Rate), y = Clearance_Rate)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = Clearance_Rate)) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "firebrick", high = "forestgreen") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Grant County Sheriff: Clearance Rates by Crime Type",
    subtitle = "Crimes with 10+ incidents; Sorted by success rate",
    x = "Type of Offense",
    y = "Percentage of Cases Cleared (Arrest Made)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```



```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(broom)

# ==========================================
# 1. DATA PREPARATION & CLEANING
# ==========================================
final_analysis_data <- da39270.0003 %>%
  filter(ORI == "WA0130000") %>%
  mutate(
    target = ifelse(RECSARR > 0, 1, 0),
    
    OFFENSE_GROUP = case_when(
      as.numeric(RECSOFS) == 1 ~ "Single Offense",
      as.numeric(RECSOFS) > 1  ~ "Multiple Offenses",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    
    SHIFT = case_when(
      as.numeric(V20111) >= 6 & as.numeric(V20111) < 14 ~ "Day",
      as.numeric(V20111) >= 14 & as.numeric(V20111) < 22 ~ "Swing",
      TRUE ~ "Graveyard"
    ),
    
    AGE_GROUP = case_when(
      as.numeric(V40181) < 18 ~ "Juvenile (<18)",
      as.numeric(V40181) >= 18 & as.numeric(V40181) < 65 ~ "Adult (18-64)",
      as.numeric(V40181) >= 65 ~ "Senior (65+)",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown Age"
    ),
    
    LOCATION_GROUP = case_when(
      V20121 %in% c("20", "01", "18") ~ "Residential/Home",
      V20121 %in% c("03", "05", "07", "08", "12", "14", "21", "24") ~ "Commercial/Retail",
      V20121 %in% c("13", "10", "16", "22") ~ "Public Space/Parking",
      TRUE ~ "Other/Specialized"
    ),
    
    CRIME = as.character(V20061)
  ) %>%
  filter(AGE_GROUP != "Unknown Age", !is.na(SHIFT))

# ==========================================
# 2. LOGISTIC REGRESSION (The Forest Plot)
# ==========================================
logit_model <- glm(target ~ OFFENSE_GROUP + SHIFT + AGE_GROUP, 
                   data = final_analysis_data, 
                   family = binomial)

model_viz_data <- tidy(logit_model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)")

forest_plot <- ggplot(model_viz_data, aes(x = estimate, y = term)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkblue") +
  labs(title = "What Predicts Case Clearance in Grant County?",
       x = "Odds Ratio (Left of Red Line = Lower Success)",
       y = "Variable") +
  theme_minimal()

# ==========================================
# 3. PREDICTIVE CRIME TABLE
# ==========================================
predictive_table <- final_analysis_data %>%
  group_by(LOCATION_GROUP, SHIFT) %>%
  mutate(sector_total = n()) %>%
  group_by(LOCATION_GROUP, SHIFT, CRIME) %>%
  summarize(Prob = n() / first(sector_total), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(LOCATION_GROUP, SHIFT) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = Prob, n = 1)

# ==========================================
# 4. VIEW RESULTS
# ==========================================
print(forest_plot)
print(predictive_table)
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(haven)
library(forcats)

viz_data <- da39270.0003 %>%
  filter(ORI == "WA0130000") %>%
  mutate(
    crime_name = as_factor(V20061),
    hour_num   = as.numeric(V20111),

    time_of_day = case_when(
      hour_num >= 0  & hour_num < 6   ~ "Late Night\n(12–6am)",
      hour_num >= 6  & hour_num < 12  ~ "Morning\n(6am–12pm)",
      hour_num >= 12 & hour_num < 18  ~ "Afternoon\n(12–6pm)",
      hour_num >= 18 & hour_num <= 23 ~ "Evening\n(6–12pm)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    time_of_day = factor(time_of_day, levels = c(
      "Late Night\n(12–6am)",
      "Morning\n(6am–12pm)",
      "Afternoon\n(12–6pm)",
      "Evening\n(6–12pm)"
    )),

    crime_name = fct_lump_n(crime_name, n = 8, other_level = "All Other Crimes")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(time_of_day))

crime_counts <- viz_data %>%
  count(time_of_day, crime_name) %>%
  group_by(time_of_day) %>%
  mutate(
    pct   = n / sum(n),
    label = paste0(round(pct * 100), "%")
  )

ggplot(crime_counts, aes(x = time_of_day, y = pct, fill = crime_name)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
  labs(
    title    = "Crime Type Distribution by Time of Day",
    subtitle = "Moses Lake, WA — proportion of each crime type per time window",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Share of Incidents (%)",
    fill     = "Crime Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.title     = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 11)
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3))

ggsave("crime_by_time.png", width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 150)
```
